subject: DevOps
title: Threat Modeling Basics
description: A 60-second lesson on Threat Modeling Basics.

hook.text: What could go wrong? Who would attack? How? Asking before coding is threat modeling.

buildup.text: Threat modeling maps data flows, trust boundaries, and attack surfaces. It shows where you're exposed.

discovery.text: STRIDE categorizes threats: Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Info leak, DoS, Privilege escalation.

twist.text: You don't need a formal method. A quick session asking 'what could an attacker do here?' helps a lot.

climax.text: Threat models evolve. New features change the attack surface. Review the model with major changes.

punchline.text: Think like an attacker before you build. Model threats early.

quiz.question: What does the STRIDE framework help with?
options: Categorizing different types of security threats
